extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won bunch sprint after RICE Michael freeloaded in breakaway and Sun-Web controlled tempo on Tawonga Gap to eliminate climber threats
tactical_patterns: Sun-Webb controlled entire race despite no sprint protected, suggesting team protecting GC rather than stage hunting opportunity
RICE Michael refused all breakaway turns but still placed fifth, exposing weak coordination among break companions lacking discipline
Mitchelton-Scott delayed sprint train until final kilometer, allowing domestique TINETTI to rejoin rather than risk early isolation
POWER Rob sacrificed GC position finishing 8:27 down to work for HINDLEY, revealing team prioritizing single GC card

rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: first win in new Mitchelton-Scott colors shows immediate adaptation, looking away from sprint suggests confidence bordering on complacency
rider_signals.RICE Michael: neutral: tactical freeloading in break enabled fifth place, questionable ethics but effective result-oriented positioning
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: negative: stage one winner couldn't match GROVES on flatter sprint finish, suggests pure power sprinter rather than versatile finisher
rider_signals.HILL Ben: negative: attacked immediately at flag drop but cramped catastrophically before final sprint, revealing overeager pacing judgment
rider_signals.POWER Rob: negative: strong climber dropped early on Tawonga then relegated to domestique duties, dramatic GC drop from top-10 shows fragile form
